allowed_adjustment_rule: Only observed pre-treatment common causes may enter the primary total-effect adjustment set. Post-treatment variables, mediators, colliders, selection variables, outcomes and treatment-definition variables are rejected before estimation.

bad_controls.reason: Offer is part of the assignment and compliance mechanism; conditioning on it changes the received-treatment question instead of adjusting baseline confounding.
Completion is caused by treatment and causes activation; controlling for it blocks part of the primary total effect.
Support chat can be caused by assistance and by high friction; conditioning on it opens non-causal paths.
Complete-case filtering after treatment changes the analysis population and can open selection paths.
The primary outcome cannot be used as an adjustment variable or feature in its own effect estimate.
A downstream outcome/descendant of activation is not a baseline control for the activation total effect.

handoff_policy.allowed_design_claim: Future estimators may use the recommended observed baseline set, but the effect claim remains blocked by unmeasured confounding from 13/03.
